title: Assessment of DAPK1 and p16 promoter methylation by bisulfite conversion followed by MSP and COBRA in human tumour cell lines
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2t3u9","text":"This protocol describes a three-session laboratory workflow for analysing promoter DNA methylation in the p16 (CDKN2A) and DAPK1 genes using sodium bisulfite conversion followed by methylation-specific PCR (MSP) and combined bisulfite restriction analysis (COBRA). Genomic DNA from four human tumour-derived cell lines (HT29, HeLa, U2OS and 143B) is used to illustrate different methylation states.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":106,"length":12},{"style":"italic","offset":123,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dq94q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cf7js","text":"Expected results include clear MSP amplification patterns specific for methylated and unmethylated DNA and interpretable COBRA restriction profiles, enabling the distinction between complete, partial and absent promoter methylation. The protocol supports structured interpretation of biologically meaningful methylation patterns as well as common sources of technical variability.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"db1f2","text":"General considerations:","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dp839","text":"All procedures should be performed on a clean laboratory bench using standard laboratory practice. Laboratory personnel must wear appropriate personal protective equipment at all times, including gloves and a lab coat.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9i69p","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8dnuu","text":"Sodium bisulfite is an irritant and should be handled with care. Preparation and handling of bisulfite solutions should be performed in a well-ventilated area, avoiding inhalation and contact with skin or eyes. Follow institutional chemical safety guidelines when handling and disposing of bisulfite-containing waste.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"43suu","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bk568","text":"Genomic DNA used in this protocol is derived from established human tumour cell lines and should be handled as biosafety level 1 (BSL-1) material when purified DNA is used. No live cells or infectious material are involved.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"l509","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ui7","text":"DNA visualization should be performed using blue light transillumination whenever possible to minimize exposure to ultraviolet radiation. Appropriate protective eyewear must be worn during gel visualization.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3qm1i","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1p0qu","text":"All chemical and biological waste must be disposed of according to institutional biosafety and waste management regulations. Reagents and samples should be stored at recommended temperatures to ensure stability and reproducibility. Equivalent reagents or equipment may be used; however, the specified products are recommended to ensure consistency with the described workflow.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"576js","text":"1. Preparation of methylated and unmethylated DNA controls (instructor-only): ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":78}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"578qo","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"561om","text":"Fully methylated DNA control: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ml6u","text":"Genomic DNA is enzymatically methylated in vitro using CpG Methyltransferase (M.SssI) (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat. No. 11368851). Briefly, genomic DNA is incubated with M.SssI in the presence of S-adenosylmethionine, allowing methylation of all cytosines within CpG dinucleotides. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":40,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f7sk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"749et","text":"Fully unmethylated DNA control: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"24pjk","text":"Unmethylated control DNA is generated by whole-genome amplification using phi29 DNA polymerase (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat. No. 10233130) and Exo-Resistant Random Primers (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat. No. 10334450). This process produces DNA lacking CpG methylation, as epigenetic marks are not transferred during in vitro synthesis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":319,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7pdp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4lou8","text":"Use of control DNA: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9mghq","text":"Both methylated and unmethylated DNA controls are used exclusively for prior validation of MSP and COBRA primer specificity and are not included in the student laboratory workflow.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bjqk7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"61hcb","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ablfc","text":"2. Genomic DNA used in the protocol (instructor-provided)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":57}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cqj1i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eovdq","text":"Genomic DNA from established human tumour-derived cell lines was used for bisulfite conversion and downstream MSP and COBRA analyses: HT29 (ATCC HTB-38), HeLa (ATCC CCL-2), U2OS (ATCC HTB-96), and 143B (ATCC CRL-8303). Genomic DNA was provided to students prior to the laboratory sessions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a7178","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f63pm","text":"3. Primer sequences used in this protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":41}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fh2r8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e84es","text":"The following oligonucleotide primers were used for methylation-specific PCR (MSP) and COBRA analyses after bisulfite conversion.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"o74b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d267a","text":"- MSP p16","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3r10n","text":"M p16 SEN: 5’- TTGTTTTCGGTTGGTGTTTTC -3’ ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"avg20","text":"M p16 ATS: 5’-CTCTTTCTTCCTCCGATACTAACG -3’","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9lokm","text":"U p16 SEN: 5’- TTGTTTTTGGTTGGTGTTTTTG -3’","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"esqcl","text":"U p16 ATS: 5’-CCTCTTTCTTCCTCCAATACTAACA -3’ ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1oshc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fb1ci","text":"-COBRA p16","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ie3p","text":"COBRA p16 SEN: 5’-GGTGGGGTTTTTATAATTAGGAAAG-3’","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cl4a","text":"COBRA p16 ATS: 5’-AAACTAAACTCCTCCCCACCTAC GGTTTGATTAGGTGTTTTGTGTTG-3’","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a1cje","text":"U DAPK1 ATS: 5’-AAAAACAATCTCTCTCCAACCTACA -3’ steps: DNA quantification
Select dsDNA measurement mode on the spectrophotometer.
Clean pedestals with Milli-Q water before and after each measurement.
Blank using 2 µL TE buffer.
Measure 2 µL of each DNA sample and record: o DNA concentration (typically 10–100 ng/µL) o An A260/280 ratio in the acceptable range (1.8–2.0) indicates adequate DNA purity.
Bisulfite conversion: Bisulfite conversion was performed according to the manufacturer's instructions using the EZ DNA Methylation Kit (Zymo Research).
In a PCR tube, combine: o 200 ng genomic DNA o 5 µL dilution buffer o Nuclease-free water to a final volume of 50 µL
Incubate at 42 °C for 15 min.
Add 100 µL conversion reagent and mix gently.
Incubate at 50 °C for 12–16 h. Include an hourly 95 °C, 1 min denaturation step to ensure DNA remains single-stranded for efficient conversion.
After completion, hold samples at 4 °C until cleanup.
Bisulfite cleanup (Zymo-Spin column)
Add 400 µL binding buffer to a Zymo-Spin column placed in a 2 mL collection tube.
Load the sample onto the column and centrifuge at ~16,000 × g (≈13,000 rpm, depending on rotor) for 30s; discard flow-through.
Add 100 µL wash buffer, centrifuge 30 s; discard flow-through.
Add 200 µL desulfonation buffer, incubate 15 min at room temperature, centrifuge 30 s; discard flow-through.
Wash twice with 150 µL wash buffer, centrifuging 30 s each time.
Transfer column to a 1.5 mL tube, add 20 µL elution buffer directly to the membrane, incubate 1 min, centrifuge 30s
Store bisulfite-treated DNA at −20 °C or proceed immediately to PCR.
PCR amplification (MSP and COBRA)
For each sample, set up three PCR reactions (50 µL each): MSP-M (methylated-specific primers) MSP-U (unmethylated-specific primers) COBRA (non-discriminatory primers)
Per 50 µL reaction: 25 µL 2× Taq PCR Master Mix 2 µL primer mix (forward + reverse, 10 µM each) 3 µL bisulfite-treated DNA Nuclease-free water to 50 µL

Cycling conditions: 95 °C for 1 min 38 cycles of: o 95 °C for 15 s o 57 °C for 15 s o 72 °C for 40 s 72 °C for 5 min Hold at 4 °C While the PCR is running,use SnapGene to visualize the p16 and DAPK1 sequences. Students must locate the primers relative to CpG islands and discuss the expected amplicons based on the chemical changes introduced by bisulfite conversion. In addition, the instructor demonstrates how to use the MethPrimer web tool to identify CpG islands and to reproduce the design of the MSP and COBRA primers employed in the practical session, so that students understand the criteria used to select primer sequences for methylated, unmethylated, and non-discriminatory assays.
Restriction digestion (COBRA) : Prepare two tubes per COBRA PCR product:
Digested sample (COBRA-D): 25 µL COBRA PCR product 3 µL 10× restriction buffer 2 µL Bsh1236I Final volume: 30 µL Incubate at 37 °C for 20 min
Undigested control (COBRA-ND): 25 µL COBRA PCR product 3 µL 10× restriction buffer 2 µL nuclease-free water Final volume: 30 µL Incubate at 37 °C for 20 min
Agarose gel preparation
Prepare a 2% agarose gel (1 g agarose in 50 mL 1× TAE buffer).
Heat until fully dissolved and allow to cool slightly.
Add SYBR Safe according to manufacturer's recommendations, pour gel, and allow to solidify.
Sample loading electrophoresis and visualization
MSP samples: mix 30 µL PCR product with 6 µL 6× loading dye
COBRA-D and COBRA-ND samples: add 6 µL 6× loading dye to each.
Load 5 µL DNA ladder (loading dye included)
Run gel at 140 V for approximately 15 min. Visualize bands using a blue-light transilluminator and document results.

Interpretation and expected student outcomes At the end of the practical session, students should be able to interpret MSP and COBRA results to determine promoter methylation status across different tumour-derived cell lines. Students are encouraged to compare MSP and COBRA results across cell lines and discuss biological versus technical sources of variability.
creator: Patricia Jiménez Cuadrado
